# awscc_resiliencehubv2_system (Resource)

Creates a system that represents a logical grouping of services.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the system.

### Optional

- `description` (String) The description of the system.
- `kms_key_id` (String) The KMS key ID for encrypting system data.
- `tags` (Attributes List) Tags assigned to the system. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tags))

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) The timestamp when the system was created.
- `id` (String) Uniquely identifies the resource.
- `system_arn` (String) The ARN of the system.
- `system_id` (String) The system ID.
- `updated_at` (String) The timestamp when the system was last updated.

<a id="nestedatt--tags"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tags`

Optional:

- `key` (String) The tag key.
- `value` (String) The tag value.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_resiliencehubv2_system.example
  identity = {
    system_arn = "system_arn"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `system_arn` (String) The ARN of the system

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_resiliencehubv2_system.example
  id = "system_arn"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_resiliencehubv2_system.example "system_arn"
```
